entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.4”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you.

A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

entry.title: SAPUI5 Tables: Maximizing efficiency through advanced Copy & Paste
entry.content:
SAP Fiori provides a modern, intelligent, and consistent user experience for SAP solutions. The technologies & tools of SAP Fiori, such as SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements, offer extensive capabilities for building, extending and running SAP's business applications. When using SAP Fiori, you'll tap into a whole world of generic features of SAP's technologies, that make your life easier. From Joule, SAP’s AI Copilot down to clever copy & paste capabilities, you'll be amazed at how you survived without them.

Advanced Copy & Paste and cell selection

SAPUI5 1.124 improves copy & paste operations in tables, letting business users work even more in a natural way with improved productivity. See these common scenarios:

1. Copy data from a table
Copy data from a table by selecting rows or a group of cells ('lasso select') and paste to a MS Excel sheet or other applications. Play with the sample application Table - CellSelector, CopyProvider.


Picture 1: Row selection

Picture 2: Cell selection

2. Paste data to a table
Paste tabular data from some source to a table in an SAPUI5 application. This typically initiates a handling of new entries, depending on the application. Use the sample application Cell Selection, Copy & Paste to test. In this context see Streamlining data entry: Exploring the new 'Paste' feature in Fiori Elements V4 tables.

3. Copy a set of values from a table to another field such as a filter field
Use sample app Smart (Responsive) Table with Criticality to try this.

 Picture 3: Copy from a cell selection

Picture 4: Paste copied cells

4. Handling split cells with multiple values
In the pictures above you see a column with combined product name and its ID. To handle this, the MS Excel export offers a 'Split cells with multiple values' option. You can achieve a similar behavior for copying data by using 'Paste Special… -> As Unicode Text' when pasting into MS Excel. (See details in Copying and Pasting from External Applications to Tables and CopyProvider.extractDataHandler.)

Picture 5: Paste Special in MS Excel

For application developers

To enable the Copy and Cell Selection features for a table in your custom application, see the details in:

  • API Reference: CopyProvider
  • API Reference: CellSelector
  • Samples: Table - CellSelector, CopyProvider

Please note: While these features are built into the Smart Table as well as the Responsive Table and Grid Table control, the availability in individual applications might depend on whether the application enabled the feature. This is the case for applications based on SAP Fiori elements and many other SAPUI5 apps.

In this context, please see details on the Spreadsheet Export. Also note that the Responsive Table received a comprehensive update of its keyboard handling.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2024 highlights on UX, application development, and AI
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2024 was amazing. It was definitely the year of AI. In addition to the usual executive keynotes, customer presentations, and inspirational speakers, every -focused session I attended included some discussion on how artificial intelligence will make the product experience better. Since my focus was on meeting customers and partners to explain our UX strategy and how to implement the SAP Fiori user experience as they transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, this summary will focus on those dimensions. You can see the full session catalog if you want to see what else was at the event, which also includes the ASUG Annual Conference.

UX Engineering solutions appeared across SAP Sapphire

It was extremely gratifying to see the solutions that our Cloud ERP UX Engineering team develops in so many sessions. My Home was in several keynotes, along with examples of SAP Fiori elements apps. The new SAP Fiori tools AI was in the SAP Build Code sessions and in the public cloud roadmap session. One of the most satisfying parts of the conference was having a gentleman introduce himself during one of the ASUG sessions on SAP S/4HANA migration. We had met at Sapphire around eight years ago and as a result of our conversation, he implemented SAP Screen Personas at his gourmet food company and it now runs in 15 factories, saving them lots of money every year.

The theme of SAP Sapphire 2024 was “Bring out your best.”

 

Unlock business agility with cloud ERP by adopting innovation more quickly

The first session I attended after the AI-heavy keynote was “Unlock business agility with cloud ERP”. Jan Gilg and Eric van Rossum discussed the evolution of ERP over the past decade. It continued the AI theme with examples of AI-driven insights and predictions of how they will change how customers use ERP to deliver new experiences to create new revenue streams. Eric also talked about different kinds of risk and how the risk of doing nothing is getting more expensive, as early adopters of AI will generate an insurmountable lead over their competitors.

One of the highlights was the demo of a new integration with Microsoft Copilot. They showed an intelligent, AI-based assistant that you can interact with via chat to locate any information in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. Ask questions in natural language and receive answers as text or charts, along with the ability to open the relevant app that contains the source data. It builds on the existing integration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud and Microsoft Teams that enables our collaborative ERP. This enhancement adds Microsoft Teams chat with an AI assistant in addition to the ability to chat with your human colleagues.

One topic in the unlock business agility session was how much the user experience improved in the last ten years.

Watch ERP169 “Unlock business agility with cloud ERP” on-demand.

 

The new ERP mindset is based on using standard processes everywhere you can

Another great SAP S/4HANA session was a panel discussion in which experts talked about “The new ERP mindset.” The mix of customer (Daniel Schumacher from Toyota Material Handling), partner (Rich Sernyak from PwC), and SAP (Eric van Rossum) vantage points provided a diverse perspective on what ERP means today. Maura Hameroff moderated.

The new ERP mindset means moving to standard processes and a more modular approach instead of using extensive customizations just because you can. Here are the key recommendations from the panelists”

  • PwC: define a clear business outcome, implement a scalable and agile platform to integrate new technologies such as AI, focus on predictability of time and cost.
  • Toyota: move from thinking about ERP for managing your current business to focusing on ERP as the platform to drive business transformation over next decade.
  • SAP: Focus on “opportunity readiness” to give your business the agility to adopt new business models and consume continuous innovation from SAP.

As both a customer of SAP S/4HANA and implementation partner, for a successful project, PwC also recommended considering which of your business processes are standard and which give you differentiated capabilities. For the former, use standard SAP; for the latter, follow clean core processes and extend your core system.

Toyota advised that you should not be afraid to disrupt your own business. Embrace disruptive technologies and try them out to understand the impact. One point Daniel made about the new ERP mindset is that what you used before successfully might not work for you tomorrow. He recommended having an open mind when you evaluate your processes. Fit to standard works in most cases. 80% of what most companies do is a commoditized process. Save your customization for what defines and differentiates your business.

Watch TRE321 “The new ERP mindset” on-demand.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition roadmap session

Wow. This was a very fast-paced session, delivered by Arpan Shah, head of product management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. He shared planned innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that are likely to ship by the end of the year. Each topic included a short demo. Sorry if I missed any; they came pretty quickly. Usual roadmap disclaimer - all of these are planned and might not make it into the product.

  • My Home will make it easier access to the applications you use most with some AI-generated recommendations.
  • Microsoft Copilot integration brings the next step of collaborative ERP with easier access to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition through Microsoft Copilot.
  • Joule copilot makes your experience easier within the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition solution by offering context-based instructions of how to complete your work more effectively.
  • Easy filter lets you intuitively filter data in SAP Fiori elements list report applications using natural language.
  • Smart summarization makes information in object pages easier to share through email or the collaboration tool of your choice.
  • Financial business insights uses AI to discover what’s meaningful within selected review booklets.
  • A new version toggle for the Manage Journal Entries app simplifies change management for new apps by letting people try out new applications without risk and the ability to switch back to the original app.
  • Intelligent recommendations for advanced returns management reduces manual effort and facilitates faster returns order creation by prefilling fields using most common scenarios and history.
  • Intelligent project recommendations for employees give you higher confidence in matching employees with projects.
  • Hands-free production order management uses natural language through voice with Joule. This is useful in environments where there are typically no keyboards.
  • AI-driven back order processing execution lets you change prioritization through the Joule natural language interface.

Watch ERP127 “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what’s next” on-demand.

 

Arpan Shah preparing to run through a rapid-fire stream of planned innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

The ASUG Hub was a focal point of conversations about SAP S/4HANA migration

As the SAP Point of contact for user experience within ASUG, I spent much of my time in the ASUG Hub, speaking with customers about UX issues, generally in the context of moving to SAP S/4HANA or bringing the SAP Fiori user experience to a larger percentage of their organization.

ASUG hosted several customer roundtables and community alliance meet-ups where ASUG members could learn from peers and interact with SAP experts about how to generate more value from their investments in SAP. Todd Lutz, Michael Giessner, and Peter Keller hosted a great discussion on best practices for migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Mohammed Siddiqui, Cristin Charbonneau, and Todd Lutz held a live town hall to understand what issues are most important to the ASUG community.

It was great to meet so many old friends and people who I knew only from online encounters in various ASUG calls or the various user groups that I host for SAP Fiori developers or business analysts that want to adapt classic UI screens using SAP Screen Personas.

Thank you to everyone that attended my session, ASUG301 in the ASUG Hub. I talked about the state of SAP Fiori today, how we got here, and where we are going by adding AI to further accelerate application development and streamline the user experience. The audience was a good mix, some customers that are using SAP Fiori throughout their enterprise, many with some SAP Fiori deployed, and a few who have not yet started their SAP Fiori journey. If you missed it, you can download the slides.

My ASUG session covered the past, present, and future of SAP Fiori

 

Leadership lessons have a longer lifetime than technology

Another great session was a panel discussion on “Commonalities, not differences: Insights in military leadership.” Daria Wick moderated the conversation with Doug Melville, and a senior military leader. Melville is author of “Invisible Generals”, where he shares his family history that includes America’s first Black generals, father and son Benjamin O. Davis Sr. and Jr., who helped integrate America’s military and create the Tuskegee Airmen.

Peter with Doug Melville, author of Invisible Generals

The panelists talked about a variety of leadership topics including teamwork, empowerment, diversity, inclusion, and how to leave a lasting legacy. The recommendation to lead with inspiration rather than intimidation moved the conversation to some funny sports references about which players you would want to coach. The take away was even the difficult players can help a team win championships. Watch The Last Dance to see what they talked about.

In another session on inclusion, Julia White, SAP’s Chief Marketing and Solutions Officer,  Ada Agrait, SAP’s SVP and Global Head of Corporate Marketing, and Margot Goodson, VP, Head of North America Diversity & Inclusion, had a panel discussion about “Strengthening underrepresented communities: partnerships and best practices.” They talked about how to bring in under-represented groups to match your organization to the market it serves. Their top recommendations:

  • Julia: rely on diverse teams or risk bringing a product to market that serves the needs of only the people on the design team
  • Ada: follow the online conversations of the people you want to recruit so you learn what’s important to them and the language they use
  • Margot: Make DEI part of your DNA so you naturally recruit diverse and inclusive teams rather than having to fill gaps later

 

These were some highlights. My full list of UX and Application Development sessions at Sapphire 2024was in my preview blog post.

My colleague, Paul Saunders, wrote an excellent summary of Sapphire on LinkedIn.

It was also great to meet so many SAP colleagues in person for the first time, including Paul, Sinit, Daria, Christian, Marco, Eric, Maura, Sid, Mark, Sibylle, Margot, Tracy, Steve, and more (apologies if your name doesn’t appear).

 

We are taking the conversation deeper at our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day

If you want to go deeper on SAP Fiori and adjacent topics, we have our next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago on Tuesday, August 13. Speakers from SAP, Mindset Consulting, Convergent IS, and Sovanta will cover what’s new in SAP’s UX portfolio, how to get started with SAP Fiori, AI innovations, the design process, and an optional hands-on workshop in which you will exercise all our tools and technologies to build an SAP Fiori app. Registration is now open.

On behalf of the SAP Cloud ERP UX Engineering team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: Adapt your app to SAPUI5 2.x - Make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 product owners and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise to directly address real-world challenges from your custom development projects.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how applications are adapted to SAPUI5 2.x

SAPUI5 is SAP’s JavaScript framework and UI library for building cross-platform, enterprise-grade Web applications. SAPUI5 has been a solid foundation for enterprise apps for more than a decade and is still in its first major version. Despite its continuous evolution and the dedicated SAPUI5 modularization project from a few years ago, SAPUI5 has remained backward compatible. However, with new requirements, such as the demand for strict Content Security Policy, as well as the evolution of major browsers (e.g. the introduction of new technologies), SAPUI5 needs to adapt. SAPUI5 is preparing for the future by leaving behind outdated concepts like synchronous network requests.

You now have the chance to understand the impact of SAPUI5 2.x on SAP’s applications, on your own applications, and on the system landscape. In this CEI project you will receive guidance, best practices, and tool support to adapt your own custom apps to SAPUI5 2.x. (If you like you can already have a look at similar details for OpenUI5 in Introducing OpenUI5 2.x.)

Project Goals and Activities

  • Validate concepts for moving existing SAPUI5 1.x apps to SAPUI5 2.x such as the necessary code changes and tool support & documentation on the code adaptation
  • Identify existing pain points
  • Gather prioritized requirements for the SAPUI5 road map with regards to new major versions

Format of Activities, Estimated Effort

Participants need to be available in the August/September 2024 timeframe for virtual sessions. Estimated effort: approx. 10h for own development activities + 6h for virtual calls:

  • Initial call (1h) in mid-August 2024 to outline the project and explain the transition to SAPUI5 2.x
  • 3 weekly calls (1h) for Q&A and feedback in August / early September
  • A final feedback call (1h) mid September to collect & prioritize feedback
  • Closing call (1h) in September


Prerequisites

This is your chance to give feedback on all aspects of moving to SAPUI5 2.x and influence the direction of the SAPUI5 road map. Join this project if

  • you are working in development projects and are familiar with SAPUI5 code
  • you have custom SAPUI5 1.x apps with freestyle SAPUI5 code, which you can adapt to SAPUI5 2.x. (This can be freestyle SAPUI5 apps or SAP Fiori elements apps with extensions / custom page.)
  • you are using your SAP Business Application Studio / VS Code environment with SAP Fiori tools.
Register for the CEI project 'SAPUI5 2.x' from June 10 - July 11, 2024.

Once the registration process is finalized, the project team invites participants for the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

entry.title: Celebrating Horizon! The new look and feel for SAP turns three
entry.content:
Happy Anniversary Horizon!

Time passed quickly and we are thrilled to celebrate the third anniversary of the Horizon visual theme for SAP Fiori - an integral part of our User Experience (UX) design. Over these years, Horizon has evolved and seamlessly integrated into SAP's product portfolio, providing our users with a simplified, personalized, and engaging user experience.

How did it start?

Born out of rigorous testing and research, Horizon has tremendously transformed how millions of users interact with SAP software. Its introduction marked a significant paradigm shift in SAP's design model and marks the entry of a revolutionary user experience across all products. Chris Allen, Head of SAP Design Systems and Language, focused on many design trends and led the core team to explore several directions. UX Designers Gerard Keane and Simone Daiber were co-leading the team of designers working on the creation of Horizon visual language foundations for web applications, including visual design specifications for UI components. The core design team worked closely with product designers representing every line of business to contribute ideas and iterate on them together. A close alignment and iterative approach with technology ensured a seamless implementation of the new Horizon visual theme – a truly collaborative effort across the entire company.

“While the core team focused on the visual design for Horizon to achieve a more, inclusive and accessible look and feel, the idea was also to give more power and flexibility to the different product areas so they could develop their own product-specific innovations as well.” (Gerard Keane, UX Designer at SAP)

Building an identity

With Horizon, the design team followed a joint vision to not only create a new face for SAP Fiori, but to connect themes and how they are set up technically. The team around Gerard and Simone worked hard to create a theming system with design tokens that enable a simple and consistent way to implement the styles adhering to the latest accessibility standards for all supported themes across all of the SAP UI technologies. The Horizon theme family consists of not only light and dark theme variants, but also high-contrast black and white to that ensure a fully inclusive user experience for all SAP customers. Check out the design token and theming design guidelines for more details.

“We were no longer handing over mockups and started to create tokens a developer could actually use.” (Gerard Keane, UX Designer at SAP)

Early design explorations for the Horizon visual theme for SAP Fiori

 

Additional 72 font styles introduced and new SAP icon styleWhile creating Horizon, the team of designers explored many design style variations and techniques to create an appealing visual theme The bespoke 72 font was expanded to include new styles to help improve typographic visual hierarchy and icons were redesigned with a bolder and more contemporary appearance matching to weights of the text used for actions.

Trial and error, disruptive ideas plus lots of feedback and iterations were involved in the process. Grey input fields seemed to be neat and tidy, however they had to go: users didn’t recognize they could actually write into those input fields, they appeared to be greyed out. Another first with Horizon was the “switch”. When Horizon was introduced, users could switch the new theme on or off again with a toggle in the header bar. This was a great way for users to get a glimpse of and get used to the new theme at their own pace.

Switching from the old theme to the new Horizon theme made easy for end users

“The best thing about Horizon is, that it could strike a spark to inspire SAP design leadership in both brand and product design to work together towards a more inclusive, shared company visual identity.” (Gerard Keane)

The Horizon was specially created to be compliant with web accessibility standards and heavily influenced SAP’s corporate branding and members of the project have been working closely with SAP Branding ever since Horizon was introduced to ensure a consistent experience for our customers and users.

Light and dark theme variation of Horizon with their color palettes

Horizon’s impact and evolution:

Our commitment to putting our users front and center, coupled with the beauty of Horizon, has ensured it’s not just about aesthetic appeal, but also about enhancing the overall efficiency and inclusivity of businesses.

To understand Horizon's impact, let's recap its innovative features and how they've brought about significant changes.

  1. Improved visualization: The Horizon visual theme offers a clean, modern interface with appealing visuals, increasing software usability. 
  2. Personalization: Horizon allows users to personalize their SAP Fiori applications, a step towards shaping applications per the user's role, tasks, and preference, thus, enhancing productivity.
  3. Responsive design: In our digitally-driven world, being device agnostic is critical. With Horizon's responsive and adaptive design, users can access SAP Fiori applications from any device – smartphone, tablet, or desktop – keeping businesses connected and agile.

With SAP S/4HANA being a frontrunner and leading example, Horizon has been at the forefront, making tasks simpler and more streamlined for users. The evolution has not stopped; instead, it continues to build on its earlier successes, contributing significantly to SAP's vision of a user-centric design approach. And our customers and users agree:

“It’s just easier to navigate what you want to see.”

“New (theme) is visually better, the colors are more friendly – (I) can imagine to use it on the iPad.”

“(The theme) is leading edge – it is modern, interesting, easy to navigate, and supportive.”

After: The Horizon theme in the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud

The Future begins today

As we step into the fourth year of Horizon's life, our aim remains unchanged – to create intuitive, business-oriented, and visually appealing software experiences. With new technologies like Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence, our commitment to enhancing our users’ experience is unwavering.

While we celebrate Horizon's third anniversary, our teams are already at work, using user feedback to further refine this ever-evolving visual theme. In fact, you can check out some of the Improvements to the Horizon Visual Theme from last year based on feedback and insights from our users. We continually aim to bridge the gap between functionality and inclusive usability, providing our users with software experiences that are as enriching as they are productive.

Finally, we’d like to express our deepest gratitude to our customers, partners, and team members who have been pivotal in making this journey successful. Here's to creating more experiences, solving more challenges, and revolutionizing more processes with Horizon.

Happy Birthday, Horizon!

entry.title: The CEI Project:Your Opportunity to Influence Availability Check in Two-Tier ERP Subsidiary Business
entry.content:
SAP's Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) platform, found at https://influence.sap.com, empowers SAP customers and partners by offering early access to SAP product developments. As a participant, you have the opportunity to interact directly with SAP product experts to influence future product direction.

This CEI project's focus is on improving real-time availability checks across a dispersed landscape, such as in Two-Tier ERP, where you have the power to influence the product.

With Availability Check in Two-Tier ERP Subsidiary Business (we call it as External Availability Check or EAC), a subsidiary can request product availability from the company's headquarters to fulfil customer demands, which can be directly confirmed to the end customers.

Participating in this project presents a unique opportunity for you to liaise with product specialists and make a significant impact on the project's outcomes.

Project objectives and activities include:

  • Evaluating ideas
  • Brainstorming use-cases
  • Discussing existing challenges and bottlenecks
  • Identifying potential alternative solutions
  • Prioritizing features and processes

The format and efforts involved in project activities are as follows:

  • Initial Calls
  • Visits to customer sites (shadowing), subject to availability
  • Workshop discussions
  • Scheduled video/teleconferences
  • End User Usability testing
  • Early prototype testing
  • Wrapping up with a closing call

To participate, you should meet the following prerequisites:

  • You are planning to, or have already implemented, a Cloud ERP for your subsidiaries
  • You are an expert or influencer, actively engaged with a dispersed landscape
  • You are a business leader well-versed in the dynamics of this process

Here is the link to register yourselves: CEI Project - EAC June 10 - July 11, 2024

Once the registration process is finalized, the project team invites participants for the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

To know more about Two-Tier ERP, kindly go through below collection of blogs:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/offerings-in-two-tier-erp-the-collection/ba-p/13522207

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402.4 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 What’s New: Leave us your Feedback!
entry.content:
We want to get your feedback on What’s New for SAP S/4HANA Cloud to make the information provided more useful for you. To do this, we are making a new commenting feature available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 in a pilot phase that starts now and will end on August 21, 2024. 

There is a new commenting feature available for the What’s New in the release SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. The feature is now available for this release as part of a pilot that will end on August 21, 2024. With this commenting feature you can leave your direct feedback to the What’s New document authors. During the pilot, authors will evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. For this, your feedback is essential! 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

In this release, we will be piloting the new Commenting Feature for What's New documentation. See this blog for more information.
